nodes.x: Moreover, brethren, I would not that ye should be ignorant, how that all our fathers were under the cloud, and all passed through the sea;
For this they willingly are ignorant of, that by the word of God the heavens were of old, and the earth standing out of the water and in the water:
And were all baptized unto Moses in the cloud and in the sea;
Our fathers understood not thy wonders in Egypt; they remembered not the multitude of thy mercies; but provoked him at the sea, even at the Red sea.
And knew not until the flood came, and took them all away; so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.
And I brought your fathers out of Egypt: and ye came unto the sea; and the Egyptians pursued after your fathers with chariots and horsemen unto the Red sea.
And were all baptized unto Moses in the cloud and in the sea;
And Moses went and spake these words unto all Israel.
And the Lord said unto Moses, Lo, I come unto thee in a thick cloud, that the people may hear when I speak with thee, and believe thee for ever. And Moses told the words of the people unto the Lord.
And Moses took the anointing oil, and anointed the tabernacle and all that was therein, and sanctified them.
And Moses went up into the mount, and a cloud covered the mount.
And Moses went into the midst of the cloud, and gat him up into the mount: and Moses was in the mount forty days and forty nights.
And did all eat the same spiritual meat;
And when he had brought them into his house, he set meat before them, and rejoiced, believing in God with all his house.
And did all drink the same spiritual drink: for they drank of that spiritual Rock that followed them: and that Rock was Christ.
And while they yet believed not for joy, and wondered, he said unto them, Have ye here any meat?
And did eat up all the herbs in their land, and devoured the fruit of their ground.
But were mingled among the heathen, and learned their works.
And did all drink the same spiritual drink: for they drank of that spiritual Rock that followed them: and that Rock was Christ.
And did all eat the same spiritual meat;

And all the thirsty drank of them,
    And were filled with wisdom,
    And their dwellings were with the righteous and holy and elect.
    2. And at that hour that Son of Man was named
    In the presence of the Lord of Spirits,
    And his name before the Head of Days.
And it came to pass that they did so, and did drink of it and were filled; and they gave unto the multitude, and they did drink, and they were filled.
Now, when the multitude had all eaten and drunk, behold, they were filled with the Spirit; and they did cry out with one voice, and gave glory to Jesus, whom they both saw and heard.
Not to all the people, but unto witnesses chosen before of God, even to us, who did eat and drink with him after he rose from the dead.
But with many of them God was not well pleased: for they were overthrown in the wilderness.
And from that time forth there were wars and bloodshed among them; but the Lord came and dwelt with his people, and they dwelt in righteousness.
For they became a righteous people; they did lay down the weapons of their rebellion, that they did not fight against God any more, neither against any of their brethren.
For there fell down many slain, because the war was of God. And they dwelt in their steads until the captivity.
¶ And they transgressed against the God of their fathers, and went a whoring after the gods of the people of the land, whom God destroyed before them.
But made his own people to go forth like sheep, and guided them in the wilderness like a flock.
Now these things were our examples, to the intent we should not lust after evil things, as they also lusted.

and lust for the things of this life, or anything more degrading and debasing in its effects, except it be the love or lust for women. As a people we believe that lust for women is, next to murder, shedding innocent blood, the most deadly of all sins. Committing whoredom or adultery destroys the man who indulges in it, and next to that, in my estimation, is the love of wealth—the lusting after…
This I say then, Walk in the Spirit, and ye shall not fulfil the lust of the flesh.
Ye lust, and have not: ye kill, and desire to have, and cannot obtain: ye fight and war, yet ye have not, because ye ask not.
So I gave them up unto their own hearts’ lust: and they walked in their own counsels.
Teaching us that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we should live soberly, righteously, and godly, in this present world;
Neither be ye idolaters, as were some of them; as it is written, The people sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to play.
What say I then? that the idol is any thing, or that which is offered in sacrifice to idols is any thing?
And they served their idols: which were a snare unto them.
For they served idols, whereof the Lord had said unto them, Ye shall not do this thing.
For all the gods of the people are idols: but the Lord made the heavens.
Yea, and they also became idolatrous, because they were deceived by the vain and flattering words of the king and priests; for they did speak flattering things unto them.
Neither let us commit fornication, as some of them committed, and fell in one day three and twenty thousand.
For this is the will of God, even your sanctification, that ye should abstain from fornication:
23 For in fornication there is neither understanding nor godliness, and all jealousy dwelleth in the lust thereof.

“Thou Shalt Not Commit Adultery” By President Milton R. Hunter Of the First Council of the Seventy We still hear the voice of Jehovah as it thundered down from Mount Sinai, commanding, “Thou shalt not commit adultery.” ( Ex. 20:14 .) For over three thousand years this commandment has reverberated throughout the Hebrew and Christian world. It has been the guideline by which millions of people have…

Israel, when in the wilderness, were a murmuring and counsel-disobeying people. The counsel of God to them was to refrain from idolatry and fornication. They disobeyed that counsel. Paul, when writing to the Corinthian Saints, says, by way of exhortation, “ Neither let us commit fornication, as some of them committed, and fell in one day twenty-andtbree thousand.” “ Neither let us tempt Christ,…
Neither let us tempt Christ, as some of them also tempted, and were destroyed of serpents.
And the poison of a serpent shall not have power to harm them.
¶ Ye shall not tempt the Lord your God, as ye tempted him in Massah.

And it came to pass that the Lord did cause the serpents that they should pursue them no more, but that they should hedge up the way that the people could not pass, that whoso should attempt to pass might fall by the poisonous serpents.

And while we were yet in the Spirit, the Lord commanded us that we should write the vision; for we beheld Satan, that old serpent, even the devil, who rebelled against God, and sought to take the kingdom of our God and his Christ—
Neither murmur ye, as some of them also murmured, and were destroyed of the destroyer.
Murmur not because of the things which thou hast not seen, for they are withheld from thee and from the world, which is wisdom in me in a time to come.
But behold, were this all we had suffered we would not murmur nor complain.

4. Murmur not because of the things which thou hast not seen, for
    they are withheld from thee and from the world, which is wisdom in
    me in a time to come.
Then a general murmur commences from these same covetous, unbelieving and disobedient saints.
Now all these things happened unto them for ensamples: and they are written for our admonition, upon whom the ends of the world are come.
All the ends of the world shall remember and turn unto the Lord: and all the kindreds of the nations shall worship before thee.

Wherefore, these things shall go from generation to generation as long as the earth shall stand; and they shall go according to the will and pleasure of God; and the nations who shall possess them shall be judged of them according to the words which are written.
For all old things shall pass away, and all things shall become new, even the heaven and the earth, and all the fulness thereof, both men and beasts, the fowls of the air, and the fishes of the sea;
For I say unto you, that this that is written must yet be accomplished in me, And he was reckoned among the transgressors: for the things concerning me have an end.
Wherefore let him that thinketh he standeth take heed lest he fall.
But if he repent not of his sins, which are unbelief and blindness of heart, let him take heed lest he fall.
Be not hasty to go out of his sight: stand not in an evil thing; for he doeth whatsoever pleaseth him.
Though he fall, he shall not be utterly cast down: for the Lord upholdeth him with his hand.
Thy words have upholden him that was falling, and thou hast strengthened the feeble knees.
Then he shall turn his face toward the fort of his own land: but he shall stumble and fall, and not be found.
There hath no temptation taken you but such as is common to man: but God is faithful, who will not suffer you to be tempted above that ye are able; but will with the temptation also make a way to…
Let no man say when he is tempted, I am tempted of God: for God cannot be tempted with evil, neither tempteth he any man:
Watch and pray, that ye enter not into temptation: the spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.
But every man is tempted, when he is drawn away of his own lust, and enticed.
For in that he himself hath suffered being tempted, he is able to succour them that are tempted.
Do this thing which I have commanded you, and you shall prosper. Be faithful, and yield to no temptation.
Wherefore, my dearly beloved, flee from idolatry.
Wherefore? because I love you not? God knoweth.
Wherefore now return, and go in peace, that thou displease not the lords of the Philistines.
Wherefore, my beloved, as ye have always obeyed, not as in my presence only, but now much more in my absence, work out your own salvation with fear and trembling.
Wherefore, let my servant Newel Knight remain with them; and as many as will go may go, that are contrite before me, and be led by him to the land which I have appointed.

Wherefore, the Lord commanded my father that he should depart into the wilderness; and the Jews also sought to take away his life; yea, and ye also have sought to take away his life; wherefore, ye are murderers in your hearts and ye are like unto them.
I speak as to wise men; judge ye what I say.
I speak to your shame. Is it so, that there is not a wise man among you? no, not one that shall be able to judge between his brethren?
Great men are not always wise: neither do the aged understand judgment.
Let men of understanding tell me, and let a wise man hearken unto me.
But I say unto you, That every idle word that men shall speak, they shall give account thereof in the day of judgment.
Hear my words, O ye wise men; and give ear unto me, ye that have knowledge.
The cup of blessing which we bless, is it not the communion of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, is it not the communion of the body of Christ?
Likewise also the cup after supper, saying, This cup is the new testament in my blood, which is shed for you.
And to administer bread and wine—the emblems of the flesh and blood of Christ—
¶ And as they were eating, Jesus took bread, and blessed it, and brake it, and gave it to the disciples, and said, Take, eat; this is my body.
For we being many are one bread, and one body: for we are all partakers of that one bread.
So we, being many, are one body in Christ, and every one members one of another.
For as the body is one, and hath many members, and all the members of that one body, being many, are one body: so also is Christ.
And when I have broken the staff of your bread, ten women shall bake your bread in one oven, and they shall deliver you your bread again by weight: and ye shall eat, and not be satisfied.
Behold Israel after the flesh: are not they which eat of the sacrifices partakers of the altar?

3. The sacrifices for sins are offered in the same manner as is the
thank-offering. But those who are unable to purchase complete
sacrifices, offer two pigeons, or turtle doves; the one of which is made
a burnt-offering to God, the other they give as food to the priests. But
we shall treat more accurately about the oblation of these creatures in
our discourse concerning sacrifices. But if a…
They sacrifice flesh for the sacrifices of mine offerings, and eat it; but the Lord accepteth them not; now will he remember their iniquity, and visit their sins: they shall return to Egypt.
They shall eat the meat offering, and the sin offering, and the trespass offering; and every dedicated thing in Israel shall be theirs.

And thou shalt offer thy burnt offerings, the flesh and the blood, upon the altar of the Lord thy God: and the blood of thy sacrifices shall be poured out upon the altar of the Lord thy God, and thou shalt eat the flesh.
¶ Thus saith the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel; Put your burnt offerings unto your sacrifices, and eat flesh.
What say I then? that the idol is any thing, or that which is offered in sacrifice to idols is any thing?
As concerning therefore the eating of those things that are offered in sacrifice unto idols, we know that an idol is nothing in the world, and that there is none other God but one.
But if any man say unto you, This is offered in sacrifice unto idols, eat not for his sake that shewed it, and for conscience sake: for the earth is the Lord’s, and the fulness thereof:

It may be asked whether I have any idols? Yes, I have most darling
idols--my God and my religion, and they are all the idols I wish to
have. 9:106.
"What help and profit have we from those idols which thou dost worship, And before which thou dost bow thyself? 1
But I say, that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice to devils, and not to God: and I would not that ye should have fellowship with devils.
They sacrificed unto devils, not to God; to gods whom they knew not, to new gods that came newly up, whom your fathers feared not.
And they shall no more offer their sacrifices unto devils, after whom they have gone a whoring. This shall be a statute for ever unto them throughout their generations.
Yea, they sacrificed their sons and their daughters unto devils,

in thy name? and in thy name have cast out devils? and in thy name done many wonderful works? “And then will I profess unto them, I never knew you: depart from me, ye that work iniquity.” Or in other words, Depart from me, I never approved of you. Who, my brethren and sisters, do you think these Scriptures refer to? Some will say to the Gentiles; but I have quite another opinion about it. There…
Ye cannot drink the cup of the Lord, and the cup of devils: ye cannot be partakers of the Lord’s table, and of the table of devils.
And it shall be, if they refuse to take the cup at thine hand to drink, then shalt thou say unto them, Thus saith the Lord of hosts; Ye shall certainly drink.

For thus saith the Lord; Behold, they whose judgment was not to drink of the cup have assuredly drunken; and art thou he that shall altogether go unpunished? thou shalt not go unpunished, but thou shalt surely drink of it.
And they said unto him, We can. And Jesus said unto them, Ye shall indeed drink of the cup that I drink of; and with the baptism that I am baptized withal shall ye be baptized:
Then took I the cup at the Lord’s hand, and made all the nations to drink, unto whom the Lord had sent me:
Do not drink wine nor strong drink, thou, nor thy sons with thee, when ye go into the tabernacle of the congregation, lest ye die: it shall be a statute for ever throughout your generations:
Do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? are we stronger than he?
How long, Lord? wilt thou be angry for ever? shall thy jealousy burn like fire?
The Lord shall go forth as a mighty man, he shall stir up jealousy like a man of war: he shall cry, yea, roar; he shall prevail against his enemies.
For thou shalt worship no other god: for the Lord, whose name is Jealous, is a jealous God:
God is jealous, and the Lord revengeth; the Lord revengeth, and is furious; the Lord will take vengeance on his adversaries, and he reserveth wrath for his enemies.
For jealousy is the rage of a man: therefore he will not spare in the day of vengeance.
All things are lawful for me, but all things are not expedient: all things are lawful for me, but all things edify not.
All things are lawful unto me, but all things are not expedient: all things are lawful for me, but I will not be brought under the power of any.
¶ But if a man be just, and do that which is lawful and right,
Is it not lawful for me to do what I will with mine own? Is thine eye evil, because I am good?
Let no man seek his own, but every man another’s wealth.

the richest people on the earth; but all he will do is to give us the ability and place means in our possession, and we must go to work and organize this means and make ourselves rich; and the first step is to stop our extravagance, cease this needless expense, learn to make that which we wear, raise that which we eat, live within ourselves, accumulate the good things of life, and so make…
Even as I please all men in all things, not seeking mine own profit, but the profit of many, that they may be saved.
Whatsoever is sold in the shambles, that eat, asking no question for conscience sake:
And whatsoever is first ripe in the land, which they shall bring unto the Lord, shall be thine; every one that is clean in thine house shall eat of it.
But whatsoever hath a blemish, that shall ye not offer: for it shall not be acceptable for you.
If any of them that believe not bid you to a feast, and ye be disposed to go; whatsoever is set before you, eat, asking no question for conscience sake.
And whatsoever hath not fins and scales ye may not eat; it is unclean unto you.
For the earth is the Lord’s, and the fulness thereof.
The earth is the Lord’s, and the fulness thereof; the world, and they that dwell therein.
And the earth also, and the power thereof, even the earth upon which you stand.
If any of them that believe not bid you to a feast, and ye be disposed to go; whatsoever is set before you, eat, asking no question for conscience sake.

They shall not offer wine offerings to the Lord, neither shall they be pleasing unto him: their sacrifices shall be unto them as the bread of mourners; all that eat thereof shall be polluted: for their bread for their soul shall not come into the house of the Lord.
And they shall eat those things wherewith the atonement was made, to consecrate and to sanctify them: but a stranger shall not eat thereof, because they are holy.
Thou shalt not also go into the house of feasting, to sit with them to eat and to drink.
But Jesus said unto them, They need not depart; give ye them to eat.
But if any man say unto you, This is offered in sacrifice unto idols, eat not for his sake that shewed it, and for conscience sake: for the earth is the Lord’s, and the fulness thereof:
For if any man see thee which hast knowledge sit at meat in the idol’s temple, shall not the conscience of him which is weak be emboldened to eat those things which are offered to idols;
And this is the law of the sacrifice of peace offerings, which he shall offer unto the Lord.
And ye shall eat it in the holy place, because it is thy due, and thy sons’ due, of the sacrifices of the Lord made by fire: for so I am commanded.

And say thou unto the people, Sanctify yourselves against to morrow, and ye shall eat flesh: for ye have wept in the ears of the Lord, saying, Who shall give us flesh to eat? for it was well with us in Egypt: therefore the Lord will give you flesh, and ye shall eat.
Conscience, I say, not thine own, but of the other: for why is my liberty judged of another man’s conscience?
Which shew the work of the law written in their hearts, their conscience also bearing witness, and their thoughts the mean while accusing or else excusing one another;)
For if I by grace be a partaker, why am I evil spoken of for that for which I give thanks?
Jesus answered him, If I have spoken evil, bear witness of the evil: but if well, why smitest thou me?
My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me? why art thou so far from helping me, and from the words of my roaring?
If it do evil in my sight, that it obey not my voice, then I will repent of the good, wherewith I said I would benefit them.
Even as it is meet for me to think this of you all, because I have you in my heart; inasmuch as both in my bonds, and in the defence and confirmation of the gospel, ye all are partakers of my grace.
Whether therefore ye eat, or drink, or whatsoever ye do, do all to the glory of God.

These things ye shall do unto the Lord in your set feasts, beside your vows, and your freewill offerings, for your burnt offerings, and for your meat offerings, and for your drink offerings, and for your peace offerings.
And there ye shall eat before the Lord your God, and ye shall rejoice in all that ye put your hand unto, ye and your households, wherein the Lord thy God hath blessed thee.

And behold, if ye will not do this I come unto you speedily; for behold, God will not suffer that we should perish with hunger; therefore he will give unto us of your food, even if it must be by the sword. Now see that ye fulfil the word of God.
And in doing these things thou wilt do the greatest good unto thy fellow beings, and wilt promote the glory of him who is your Lord.
And ye shall eat in plenty, and be satisfied, and praise the name of the Lord your God, that hath dealt wondrously with you: and my people shall never be ashamed.
Give none offence, neither to the Jews, nor to the Gentiles, nor to the church of God:
And the first preacher of this church unto the church, and before the world, yea, before the Gentiles; yea, and thus saith the Lord God, lo, lo! to the Jews also. Amen.
While he answered for himself, Neither against the law of the Jews, neither against the temple, nor yet against Cæsar, have I offended any thing at all.
Wherefore, these things go forth from the Jews in purity unto the Gentiles, according to the truth which is in God.
Even as I please all men in all things, not seeking mine own profit, but the profit of many, that they may be saved.
For though I be free from all men, yet have I made myself servant unto all, that I might gain the more.
Let no man seek his own, but every man another’s wealth.
For what shall it profit a man, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul?
